plan_text:
# JP’s Confirmation Reception — Master Plan

Saturday, May 16, 2026 | St. Mary of the Immaculate Conception | Reception at home after Mass

-----

## NOW — This Week (by April 30)

- [ ] Order JP’s Confirmation cake. Custom inscription (e.g. “Congratulations JP — Confirmed in the Faith”). Holy Spirit or cross design. Confirm pickup for May 15 or morning of May 16. Get deposit receipt. DEADLINE: April 30.
- [ ] Begin shopping for JP’s special gift from mom and dad. Ideas: quality rosary, patron saint medal, leather-bound Bible or missal, cross necklace, meaningful keepsake. If ordering online, allow 2 weeks for shipping. DEADLINE: Order placed by April 30.
- [ ] Send invitations. RSVP deadline is May 6. Include home address for reception. Note pollen/allergy accommodations for friend if needed. DEADLINE: April 30.

-----

## EARLY MAY — Outfit & Gift (May 1–7)

- [ ] Take JP to shop for a suit. Navy, charcoal, or black. Fit the shoulders first — hardest to alter. Try JCPenney, Men’s Wearhouse, or Target. DEADLINE: May 1–3.
- [ ] Complete JP’s outfit: white or light blue dress shirt, tie (consider red — JP’s color), black/brown leather dress shoes, matching belt, dark dress socks. Do a full dress rehearsal at home. DEADLINE: By May 5.
- [ ] Finalize and order/purchase JP’s special gift. If online, order by May 5. Wrap and store safely. Plan reveal moment — during cake is ideal. DEADLINE: By May 5.
- [ ] Collect RSVPs. Follow up with non-responders May 7. Finalize headcount for food planning. RSVP deadline: May 6. Follow-up deadline: May 7.

-----

## MID MAY — House Prep (May 8–12)

- [ ] Shampoo the sofas. Allow 3–4 full days to dry. Use upholstery shampoo or rented machine. Fan on cushions for airflow afterward. Sniff-check the day before. DEADLINE: By May 12.
- [ ] Inflate and test bounce house. Check for slow leaks. Confirm blower motor and extension cord work. Measure basement space now in case it needs to go inside. DEADLINE: By May 10.
- [ ] Deep-clean entertaining spaces: living room, dining area, kitchen, guest bathroom. Wash and press any table linens. DEADLINE: May 10–12.
- [ ] Plan food layout and table setup. Decide placement of apps table, jambalaya station, dessert table. Pull out all serving dishes, platters, tongs, utensils — confirm you have everything. DEADLINE: By May 12.
- [ ] Plan pollen-control strategy. Identify all windows/doors to close morning of party. Set HVAC to recirculate/indoor air mode. Consider portable HEPA purifier in main room. Text friend with your plan. DEADLINE: Plan in place by May 12.

-----

## LATE WEEK — Food Prep (May 13–15)

- [ ] Main grocery run. Buy: jambalaya ingredients (andouille, shrimp or chicken, trinity veg, rice, broth, spices), rolls, cheese plate (2–3 cheeses, crackers, grapes, honey, nuts, fig jam), fruit plate produce, veggie plate produce and dip, drinks, ice, napkins. DEADLINE: May 14–15.
- [ ] Pick up the cake. Confirm bakery pickup time. Clear refrigerator shelf. Transport level. DEADLINE: May 15.
- [ ] Partial prep the night before (May 15 evening): wash/cut all produce, pre-arrange cheese boards (covered, no crackers yet), prep fruits that won’t brown. Set all serving boards out on counter for fast morning assembly.
- [ ] Make the jambalaya May 15 evening. Refrigerate overnight. Reheat May 16 with a splash of broth — flavors deepen overnight. Makes morning much easier.
- [ ] Final house tidy and setup (May 15 evening): vacuum, wipe kitchen, set out all serving dishes in position, set up drinks station with cups/ice/labels, stock guest bathroom (soap, towels, TP).
- [ ] Check pollen forecast for May 16. Decide: bounce house outside or basement. Text friend the update. DEADLINE: May 15.

-----

## MORNING OF MAY 16 — Final Prep

- [ ] Close all windows and doors. Set HVAC to recirculate/indoor air only. Do this first thing before opening anything. BY: 9 AM.
- [ ] Set up bounce house in chosen location (basement or backyard). Inflate, confirm blower running steadily, clear safety perimeter. BY: 10 AM.
- [ ] Reheat jambalaya on low with splash of broth. Finish assembling all plates (add crackers to cheese board now). Warm rolls in foil at 200°F. Everything guest-ready by noon. BY: 11:30 AM.
- [ ] Get family dressed. JP in full suit — take photos at home before leaving. Allow 45 minutes. BY: 12:15 PM.
- [ ] Place JP’s wrapped gift somewhere special for the reveal moment. BY: Before leaving.
- [ ] Leave for St. Mary of the Immaculate Conception. BY: 12:30 PM.

-----

## DAY-OF TIMELINE

9:00 AM — Close windows, set HVAC, set up bounce house
10:00 AM — Reheat jambalaya, finish all plates, warm rolls
11:30 AM — Food ready; family gets dressed; photos of JP
12:30 PM — Leave for church
1:30 PM — Confirmation Mass at St. Mary of the Immaculate Conception
~3:30 PM — Return home; guests arrive; food spread open
~5:00 PM — Cake + JP’s gift moment
~6:00 PM — Wind down; guests depart

-----

## FOOD LIST

Apps/Grazing:

- Cheese plate: 2–3 cheeses, crackers, grapes, honey, nuts, fig jam
- Fruit plate: strawberries, grapes, melon, berries
- Veggie plate: assorted vegetables with ranch or hummus

Main:

- Jambalaya (large pot, ~10 qt for under 20 guests)
- Rolls (warmed)

Dessert:

- Congratulations cake for JP

Drinks (pick 2–3):

- Lemonade, sparkling water, sweet tea, coffee/tea for adults

-----

## JP’S OUTFIT CHECKLIST

- [ ] Suit (navy, charcoal, or black)
- [ ] Dress shirt (white or light blue)
- [ ] Tie (red or classic stripe)
- [ ] Dress shoes (black or brown leather)
- [ ] Belt (matching shoes)
- [ ] Dark dress socks
- [ ] Full dress rehearsal done at home before May 16

plan_text:
Convene ✨
**Lorenzo:** This plan beautifully centers food as an act of love and celebration for JP's sacrament — the jambalaya made the night before, the custom cake with its faith inscription, the thoughtful cheese and fruit spreads all create a feast worthy of this milestone. I'd suggest we think about the meal not just as party food but as a true reception table where guests can linger and celebrate what Confirmation means. Perhaps we set the cake in a place of honor with JP's candle from the Mass nearby, making the reveal moment feel sacred as well as celebratory. **Coach:** The week leading up to Confirmation is packed with indoor tasks — cleaning, shopping, prep — so I want to make sure the boys, especially Joseph and Michael, get outside time to burn energy and stay regulated. I'd recommend we protect a few outdoor windows: maybe Joseph helps inflate and test the bounce house on May 10 as active play, and the morning of May 16 we do a short family walk before getting dressed. This keeps everyone grounded in their bodies so they can be fully present for the Mass and celebration. **Dr. Monica:** For James at 13 months, a 1:30 PM Mass lands right in typical nap territory, so Lauren should plan for him to nap earlier that morning or accept he may be drowsy or fussy during the service — having a quiet exit strategy is wise. Michael at 5 can participate meaningfully if we prepare him ahead of time: explain what Confirmation is in simple terms, maybe read a picture book about the Holy Spirit, so he feels included rather than just told to sit still. Both little ones will do better at the reception if the bounce house is ready as an immediate outlet after the more formal church time. **Lucy's Synthesis:** Lauren, this plan is already beautifully ordered around what matters most — JP's step deeper into his faith, witnessed and celebrated by the people who love him. I'd encourage you to add a few intentional faith touches: perhaps a family prayer the night before as you finish the jambalaya together, asking the Holy Spirit to prepare JP's heart. On the morning of May 16, before the rush of getting dressed, gather everyone briefly to pray over JP — this grounds the whole day in its true purpose. Consider placing his Confirmation candle on the food table near the cake, creating a visual reminder that this feast flows from the altar. The gift reveal during cake time is a perfect moment to speak words of blessing over him — let John or both of you share what you hope for his life in the Spirit. Everything else — the clean house, the food, the bounce house for the little ones — serves this sacred center. You've built a plan that honors both the sacrament and your family's real life; trust it, and let the Holy Spirit do the rest.

plan_text:
Week 1 (now — this week)
Day 1: Buy supplies: cardboard (free), 5 bags compost/topsoil mix, 1 lb blood meal, 3 lbs bone meal. Lay cardboard directly on your chosen 4x8 area, overlapping edges 6 inches so weeds can’t push through. Wet it thoroughly.
Day 2-3: Mix blood meal and bone meal into your compost/topsoil. Layer 4-6 inches of that mixture on top of the wet cardboard. Water it all thoroughly. Then leave it alone for 5-7 days minimum. Blood meal needs to mellow or it’ll burn roots.
Week 2: Buy transplants, not seeds — you’ve lost the window for direct seeding tomatoes and peppers. Look for transplants that are stocky and short, not tall and leggy. Leggy means stressed already. Set up your trellis or cucumber support structure before planting — doing it after damages roots. Check soil temp with a cheap thermometer. Don’t plant until it reads 65°F consistently.
Planting day (ideally day 10-14 from now): Cucumbers on the north end with trellis. 2 tomatoes center, caged. 2-3 peppers south end (they like heat, benefit from tomato shade later in summer). Tomatoes: bury them deep, up to the first set of leaves — they root along the buried stem. Peppers: plant at same depth as their pot, no deeper. Cucumbers: plant at same depth, handle roots gently, they hate disturbance. Water in with diluted fish emulsion immediately after planting.
Weeks 3-4: Water consistently — these crops hate wet/dry cycles. 1 inch per week minimum, more in heat. Mulch around plants with straw or wood chips once they’re established (3-5 days after planting). Don’t fertilize again yet. Let them establish.
Week 6 (when flowers appear): Side-dress with blood meal lightly around each plant. Switch to a low-nitrogen, higher phosphorus liquid fertilizer (look for something like 5-10-5) to support fruit set rather than leaf growth. This is where most home gardeners go wrong — they keep feeding nitrogen and get huge plants with little fruit.
Ongoing through summer: Tomatoes: pinch suckers weekly, keep to 1-2 main stems. Cucumbers: harvest aggressively — leaving overripe cucumbers on the vine signals the plant to stop producing. Peppers: largely leave alone, they’re slow and don’t like fussing.
Year one on unknown ground with a lasagna bed is somewhat experimental. You may hit drainage issues, compaction below the cardboard, or weed pressure from edges. The bed will be significantly better in year two once the cardboard breaks down and your amendments have cycled through fully.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

plan_text: Father Gregory:** The plan wisely suggests JP could help with construction, but I'd make that concrete now rather than leaving it as a vague "consider" item. This is a perfect applied science opportunity—soil chemistry, plant biology, even geometry for the bed dimensions. I'd also suggest having Joseph document the project with a simple garden journal; tracking growth over the summer makes excellent nature study. **Lorenzo:** From a kitchen perspective, I'd want to know *which varieties* you're planting—a Roma tomato serves very differently than a Cherokee Purple, and pickling cucumbers versus slicers changes how I'd plan summer meals. Also, consider adding one basil plant near the tomatoes; they're companion plants and you'll want fresh basil when those tomatoes come in. Don't skip the fish emulsion step—it really does make a difference for transplant survival. **Coach:** The physical work today is real—hauling compost bags, laying and wetting cardboard. With James at 13 months and Michael at 5, Lauren needs a plan for *when* she'll do this. I'd suggest late afternoon when John's home, or build it into outdoor time where the boys can dig in nearby dirt while she works. Don't underestimate the bending and lifting; pace yourself and hydrate. **Dr. Monica:** Keep Michael involved but set clear boundaries around the blood meal and bone meal—those amendments aren't toxic in small amounts but shouldn't be handled or tasted. James will want to be in the dirt, which is actually wonderful for his immune development, but he'll need a contained play area away from the work zone. A pack-n-play outside or John on baby duty makes this manageable. **Lucy's Synthesis:** Lauren, your plan is solid—you're not missing structural steps. What you're missing is the *who and when* for today's task. Before you head to the store, decide: Is this a late-afternoon project when John's home with the littles, or are you bringing all four boys and building in extra patience time? I'd assign JP to help carry bags and lay cardboard, give Joseph the garden journal idea Father Gregory mentioned, and add basil to your transplant list for May 4th. The only thing you might genuinely regret skipping is noting your specific tomato and cucumber varieties—write them down so Lorenzo can plan meals and you can repeat what works next year.

Weeks 3-4: Water consistently — these crops hate wet/dry cycles. 1 inch per week minimum, more in heat. Mulch around plants with straw or wood chips once they’re established (3-5 days after planting). Don’t fertilize again yet. Let them establish.
Week 6 (when flowers appear): Side-dress with blood meal lightly around each plant. Switch to a low-nitrogen, higher phosphorus liquid fertilizer (look for something like 5-10-5) to support fruit set rather than leaf growth. This is where most home gardeners go wrong — they keep feeding nitrogen and get huge plants with little fruit.
Ongoing through summer: Tomatoes: pinch suckers weekly, keep to 1-2 main stems. Cucumbers: harvest aggressively — leaving overripe cucumbers on the vine signals the plant to stop producing. Peppers: largely leave alone, they’re slow and don’t like fussing.
Year one on unknown ground with a lasagna bed is somewhat experimental. You may hit drainage issues, compaction below the cardboard, or weed pressure from edges. The bed will be significantly better in year two once the cardboard breaks down and your amendments have cycled through fully

---

**9:00 AM — JUSTICE (Family Strength + Skill Block)**
**Duration:** 20-30 minutes  
**Location:** Home (living room, stairs, or yard)

**YOUR WORKOUT (scaled for late luteal phase — Day 24):**

*Warm-up (3 min):*
- 30sec arm circles
- 30sec leg swings
- 1min easy movement (walk, sway, light stretching)

*Main Circuit (2 sets x 12-15 reps — lighter load, focus on form):*

**Core work:**
- Bird dogs: 12 reps per side (alternate legs, hold 1sec)
- Dead bugs: 12 reps (slow, controlled)
- Side planks: 20-30sec hold each side

**Lower body:**
- Squats: 12-15 reps (bodyweight, chest up)

**Pelvic floor activation:**

- Glute squeezes: 10 reps (2sec hold, 2sec release)

*Cool-down (2-3 min):*
- Child's pose: 30sec
- Spinal twist: 30sec each side
- Hip opener (figure-4 stretch): 30sec each side

---

**WHY THIS MATTERS TODAY:**

You're at **Day 24 of your cycle** (late luteal phase). Your energy is naturally declining. This is **not** a "push hard" day — it's a **form-focused, strength-maintenance day**. Lighter loads, more reps, intentional movement.

**The boys do this WITH you** (scaled for their ages). JP leads some reps. Joseph participates. Michael does his own version. You're moving together.

plan_text:
**YOUR 6-WEEK POSTPARTUM CORE & RESILIENCE BLOCK**
**Organized by Day of the Week**

---

## **MONDAY — FORTITUDE (Family Run)**

**Time:** 11:00 AM  
**Duration:** 28 minutes total  
**Location:** Outdoor (track, neighborhood, or park)  
**Participants:** You + JP (runs 1.5-2mi) + Joseph (runs 1-1.5mi) + Michael (bikes/scoots) + James (stroller)

**Structure:**
- **Warm-up (5 min):** Easy pace, conversation-level intensity
- **Main Work (18 min):** Run/walk intervals
  - Alternate: 2 min running, 1 min walking (repeat 6x)
  - Focus: Ankle loading (rebuilds stability post-pregnancy) + core engagement (maintain posture while running)
- **Cool-down (5 min):** Walk together, breathe deeply, reconnect as a family

**How to Adjust by Cycle Phase:**

*Follicular Phase (Days 1-14):*
- Push the intervals. Run longer, walk shorter.
- Aim for 2:30 min run / 0:45 min walk if you feel strong.

*Luteal Phase (Days 15-28):*
- Extend the warm-up to 7 min. Reduce intensity.
- Aim for 1:45 min run / 1:15 min walk.
- Days 23-28 (late luteal): Consider a brisk walk with light jog intervals instead.

**Post-Workout Log:**
- Time started: ___
- Run/walk ratio today: ___
- Ankle/knee/core how it felt: ___
- Energy level after: ___

---

## **WEDNESDAY — JUSTICE (Family Strength + Skill Block)**

**Time:** 9:00 AM  
**Duration:** 20-30 minutes  
**Location:** Home (living room, stairs, or yard)  
**Participants:** You + JP + Joseph + Michael (all scaled to age/ability)

**Warm-up (3 min):**
- 30sec arm circles (both directions)
- 30sec leg swings (forward/back, side-to-side)
- 1 min easy movement (walk, sway, light stretching)

**Main Circuit — 2 Sets**

*Follicular & Ovulatory (Days 1-14): 3 sets x 10-12 reps, heavier load*  
*Luteal (Days 15-28): 2 sets x 12-15 reps, lighter load, focus on form*

**Core Work:**
1. **Bird Dogs**
   - 12-15 reps per side (alternate legs, hold 1sec at top)
   - Form: Hands under shoulders, knees under hips. Extend opposite arm + leg, squeeze core.

2. **Dead Bugs**
   - 12-15 reps (slow, controlled 3-second lower)
   - Form: Lie on back, arms to ceiling, knees bent at 90°. Lower opposite arm + leg without arching back.

3. **Side Planks**
   - 20-30sec hold each side
   - Form: Elbow under shoulder, body in straight line. Squeeze obliques.

**Lower Body:**
1. **Squats**
   - 12-15 reps (bodyweight, chest up, knees tracking over toes)
   - Form: Hips back, weight in heels, full range of motion.

2. **Lunges**
   - 12-15 reps per leg (alternating forward lunges)
   - Form: Step forward, lower back knee toward ground, push through front heel to stand.

3. **Step-ups**
   - 12-15 reps per leg (using stairs or low bench)
   - Form: Step up, drive through front heel, squeeze glute at top.

**Upper Body:**
1. **Push-ups**
   - 12-15 reps (incline on counter/couch, or wall push-ups — modify as needed)
   - Form: Hands shoulder-width, body in straight line, lower chest to surface, press back up.

2. **Rows**
   - 12-15 reps (resistance band or towel in doorframe, slow 2-second lower)
   - Form: Pull elbows back, squeeze shoulder blades, control the return.

**Pelvic Floor Activation (CRITICAL — 13 months postpartum):**
1. **Bridges**
   - 12 reps (2sec hold at top, 1sec lower)
   - Form: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat. Drive through heels, squeeze glutes, lift hips. Pause. Lower.

2. **Glute Squeezes**
   - 10 reps (2sec hold, 2sec release)
   - Form: Standing or sitting. Squeeze glutes hard

The post-workout logging sections could be turned into simple daily tasks to track progress and adjust intensity based on how Lauren feels.
Since this involves all family members, consider checking for conflicts with existing homeschool schedule and other commitments on Mondays and Wednesdays.

plan_text:
Good afternoon,

I hope this message reaches the parents of John Paul McAdams.

I wanted to share some important information as we approach the end of the Religious Education year. It has been a pleasure teaching this class—John Paul has been a wonderful student, and I’ve enjoyed all of the conversations we have had.

Our final class will take place this Tuesday, May 5, 2026, at 4:45 PM, and we will celebrate with a pizza party. If you would like to send John Paul with drinks or snacks to share, you are welcome to do so.

Immediately following class, at 6:00 PM, there will be a Confirmation rehearsal in the main church, running until 7:00 PM.

I have also invited John Paul to attend High School Youth Group this Sunday, May 3, 2026. The group meets in the PLC (the same location as our class) from 6:15–8:30 PM. This is a great opportunity for him to build friendships with other Catholic teens while enjoying dinner, games, and faith formation.

John Paul informed me that he is usually busy with family dinner on Sundays. I encourage you to let John Paul go at least once to see if he likes it (The last youth group of the year is this Sunday).

I will make sure to introduce John Paul to the other adult leaders, as well as some of the other boys at youth group who share similar interests.

If you have any questions or concerns as we wrap up the year, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

Sam Rotsch
540-604-7691

# Holy Spirit Novena for JP's Confirmation
**Source:** Holy Ghost Fathers, 1912 (EWTN, Imprimatur granted)
**Dates:** May 6–14, 2026 | **Confirmation:** May 16, 2026

---

## HOW TO USE THIS WITH YOUR AI PLANNER

Paste this entire document and add this instruction:

> "Create 9 calendar events from May 6–14, 2026, one per day, at 7:00 AM. Title each event using the event title listed for that day. Paste the full text for that day — meditation, prayer, and daily prayers — into the event description."

---

## PRAYERS SAID EVERY DAY

Recite these after each day's meditation and prayer, in this order:
1. Our Father × 1
2. Hail Mary × 1
3. Glory Be × 7
4. Act of Consecration (below)
5. Prayer for the Seven Gifts (below)

---

### Act of Consecration to the Holy Ghost
On my knees before the great multitude of heavenly witnesses, I offer myself, soul and body to You, Eternal Spirit of God. I adore the brightness of Your purity, the unerring keenness of Your justice, and the might of Your love. You are the Strength and Light of my soul. In You I live and move and am. I desire never to grieve You by unfaithfulness to grace, and I pray with all my heart to be kept from the smallest sin against You. Mercifully guard my every thought and grant that I may always watch for Your light, and listen to Your voice, and follow Your gracious inspirations. I cling to You and give myself to You, and ask You by Your compassion to watch over me in my weakness. Holding the pierced Feet of Jesus and looking at His Five Wounds, and trusting in His Precious Blood and adoring His opened Side and stricken Heart, I implore You, Adorable Spirit, Helper of my infirmity, so to keep me in Your grace that I may never sin against You. Give me grace, O Holy Ghost, Spirit of the Father and the Son, to say to You always and everywhere: "Speak, Lord, for Your servant heareth." Amen.

---

### Prayer for the Seven Gifts of the Holy Ghost
O Lord Jesus Christ, Who, before ascending into heaven, did promise to send the Holy Ghost to finish Your work in the souls of Your Apostles and Disciples, deign to grant the same Holy Spirit to me that He may perfect in my soul the work of Your grace and Your love. Grant me the Spirit of Wisdom, that I may despise the perishable things of this world and aspire only after the things that are eternal; the Spirit of Understanding, to enlighten my mind with the light of Your divine truth; the Spirit of Counsel, that I may ever choose the surest way of pleasing God and gaining heaven; the Spirit of Fortitude, that I may bear my cross with You, and that I may overcome with courage all the obstacles that oppose my salvation; the Spirit of Knowledge, that I may know God and know myself, and grow perfect in the science of the Saints; the Spirit of Piety, that I may find the service of God sweet and amiable; the Spirit of Fear, that I may be filled with a loving reverence towards God, and may dread in any way to displease Him. Mark me, dear Lord, with the sign of Your true disciples, and animate me in all things with Your Spirit. Amen.

---

## THE NINE DAYS

---

### DAY 1 — May 6
**Event title:** Novena for JP – Day 1: The Holy Ghost

*Holy Spirit! Lord of Light!
From Your clear celestial height,
Your pure beaming radiance give!*

**Meditation:**
Only one thing is important — eternal salvation. Only one thing, therefore, is to be feared — sin. Sin is the result of ignorance, weakness, and indifference. The Holy Ghost is the Spirit of Light, of Strength, and of Love. With His sevenfold gifts He enlightens the mind, strengthens the will, and inflames the heart with love of God. To ensure our salvation we ought to invoke the Divine Spirit daily, for "The Spirit helpeth our infirmity. We know not what we should pray for as we ought. But the Spirit Himself asketh for us."

**Prayer:**
Almighty and eternal God, Who hast vouchsafed to regenerate us by water and the Holy Ghost, and hast given us forgiveness of all sins, vouchsafe to send forth from heaven upon us Thy sevenfold Spirit: the Spirit of Wisdom and Understanding, the Spirit of Counsel and Fortitude, the Spirit of Knowledge and Piety, and fill us with the Spirit of Holy Fear. Amen.

*Our Father × 1 | Hail Mary × 1 | Glory Be × 7 | Act of Consecration | Prayer for the Seven Gifts*

---

### DAY 2 — May 7
**Event title:** Novena for JP – Day 2: Gift of Fear of the Lord

*Come, Father of the poor!
Come, treasures which endure!
Come, Light of all that live!*

**Meditation:**
The gift of Fear fills us with a sovereign respect for God, and makes us dread nothing so much as to offend Him by sin. It is a fear that arises not from the thought of hell, but from sentiments of reverence and filial submission to our heavenly Father. It is the fear that is the beginning of wisdom, detaching us from worldly pleasures that could in any way separate us from God. "They that fear the Lord will prepare their hearts, and in His sight will sanctify their souls."

**Prayer:**
Come, O blessed Spirit of Holy Fear, penetrate my inmost heart, that I may set You, my Lord and God, before my face forever; help me to shun all things that can offend You, and make me worthy to appear before the pure eyes of Your Divine Majesty in heaven, where You live and reign in the unity of the ever Blessed Trinity, God world without end. Amen.

*Our Father × 1 | Hail Mary × 1 | Glory Be × 7 | Act of Consecration | Prayer for the Seven Gifts*

---

### DAY 3 — May 8
**Event title:** Novena for JP – Day 3: Gift of Piety

*Thou, of all consolers best,
Visiting the troubled breast,
Dost refreshing peace bestow.*

**Meditation:**
The gift of Piety begets in our hearts a filial affection for God as our most loving Father. It inspires us to love and respect for His sake persons and things consecrated to Him, as well as those who are vested with His authority — His Blessed Mother and the Saints, the Church and its visible Head, our parents and superiors, our country and its rulers. He who is filled with the gift of Piety finds the practice of his religion not a burdensome duty, but a delightful service. Where there is love, there is no labor.

**Prayer:**
Come, O Blessed Spirit of Piety, possess my heart. Enkindle therein such

plan_text:
HOLY SPIRIT NOVENA FOR JP’S CONFIRMATION
Source: Holy Ghost Fathers, 1912 (EWTN, Imprimatur granted)
Dates: May 6-14, 2026  Confirmation: May 16, 2026
HOW TO USE THIS WITH YOUR AI PLANNER
Paste this entire document and add this instruction:
“Create 9 calendar events from May 6-14, 2026, one per day, at 7:00 AM. Title each event using the event title listed for that day. Paste the full text for that day into the event description, including the meditation, prayer, and a reminder to pray the daily closing prayers.”
PRAYERS SAID EVERY DAY
Recite these after each day’s meditation and prayer, in this order:
	1.	Our Father, once
	2.	Hail Mary, once
	3.	Glory Be, seven times
	4.	Act of Consecration
	5.	Prayer for the Seven Gifts
ACT OF CONSECRATION TO THE HOLY GHOST
On my knees before the great multitude of heavenly witnesses, I offer myself, soul and body to You, Eternal Spirit of God. I adore the brightness of Your purity, the unerring keenness of Your justice, and the might of Your love. You are the Strength and Light of my soul. In You I live and move and am. I desire never to grieve You by unfaithfulness to grace, and I pray with all my heart to be kept from the smallest sin against You. Mercifully guard my every thought and grant that I may always watch for Your light, and listen to Your voice, and follow Your gracious inspirations. I cling to You and give myself to You, and ask You by Your compassion to watch over me in my weakness. Holding the pierced Feet of Jesus and looking at His Five Wounds, and trusting in His Precious Blood and adoring His opened Side and stricken Heart, I implore You, Adorable Spirit, Helper of my infirmity, so to keep me in Your grace that I may never sin against You. Give me grace, O Holy Ghost, Spirit of the Father and the Son, to say to You always and everywhere: Speak, Lord, for Your servant heareth. Amen.
PRAYER FOR THE SEVEN GIFTS OF THE HOLY GHOST
O Lord Jesus Christ, Who, before ascending into heaven, did promise to send the Holy Ghost to finish Your work in the souls of Your Apostles and Disciples, deign to grant the same Holy Spirit to me that He may perfect in my soul the work of Your grace and Your love. Grant me the Spirit of Wisdom, that I may despise the perishable things of this world and aspire only after the things that are eternal; the Spirit of Understanding, to enlighten my mind with the light of Your divine truth; the Spirit of Counsel, that I may ever choose the surest way of pleasing God and gaining heaven; the Spirit of Fortitude, that I may bear my cross with You, and that I may overcome with courage all the obstacles that oppose my salvation; the Spirit of Knowledge, that I may know God and know myself, and grow perfect in the science of the Saints; the Spirit of Piety, that I may find the service of God sweet and amiable; the Spirit of Fear, that I may be filled with a loving reverence towards God, and may dread in any way to displease Him. Mark me, dear Lord, with the sign of Your true disciples, and animate me in all things with Your Spirit. Amen.
THE NINE DAYS
DAY 1 - May 6
Event title: Novena for JP - Day 1: The Holy Ghost
Holy Spirit, Lord of Light, from Your clear celestial height, Your pure beaming radiance give.
Meditation:
Only one thing is important - eternal salvation. Only one thing, therefore, is to be feared - sin. Sin is the result of ignorance, weakness, and indifference. The Holy Ghost is the Spirit of Light, of Strength, and of Love. With His sevenfold gifts He enlightens the mind, strengthens the will, and inflames the heart with love of God. To ensure our salvation we ought to invoke the Divine Spirit daily, for “The Spirit helpeth our infirmity. We know not what we should pray for as we ought. But the Spirit Himself asketh for us.”
Prayer:
Almighty and eternal God, Who hast vouchsafed to regenerate us by water and the Holy Ghost, and hast given us forgiveness of all sins, vouchsafe to send forth from heaven upon us Thy sevenfold Spirit: the Spirit of Wisdom and Understanding, the Spirit of Counsel and Fortitude, the Spirit of Knowledge and Piety, and fill us with the Spirit of Holy Fear. Amen.
Close with: Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.
DAY 2 - May 7
Event title: Novena for JP - Day 2: Gift of Fear of the Lord
Come, Father of the poor, come, treasures which endure, come, Light of all that live.
Meditation:
The gift of Fear fills us with a sovereign respect for God, and makes us dread nothing so much as to offend Him by sin. It is a fear that arises not from the thought of hell, but from sentiments of reverence and filial submission to our heavenly Father. It is the fear that is the beginning of wisdom, detaching us from worldly pleasures that could in any way separate us from God. “They that fear the Lord will prepare their hearts, and in His sight will sanctify their souls.”
Prayer:
Come, O blessed Spirit of Holy Fear, penetrate my inmost heart, that I may set You, my Lord and God, before my face forever; help me to shun all things that can offend You, and make me worthy to appear before the pure eyes of Your Divine Majesty in heaven, where You live and reign in the unity of the ever Blessed Trinity, God world without end. Amen.
Close with: Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.
DAY 3 - May 8
Event title: Novena for JP - Day 3: Gift of Piety
Thou, of all consolers best, visiting the troubled breast, dost refreshing peace bestow.
Meditation:
The gift of Piety begets in our hearts a filial affection for God as our most loving Father. It inspires us to love and respect for His sake persons and things consecrated to Him, as well as those who are vested with His authority - His Blessed Mother and the Saints, the Church and its visible Head, our parents and superiors, our country and its rulers. He who is filled with the gift of Piety finds the practice of his religion not a burdensome duty, but a delightful service. Where there is love, there is no labor.
Prayer:
Come, O Blessed Spirit of Piety, possess my heart. Enkindle therein such a love for God, that I may find satisfaction only in His service, and for His sake lovingly submit to all legitimate authority. Amen.
Close with: Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.
DAY 4 - May 9
Event title: Novena for JP - Day 4: Gift of Fortitude
Thou in toil art comfort sweet, pleasant coolness in the heat, solace in the midst of woe.
Meditation:
By the gift of Fortitude the soul is strengthened against natural fear, and supported to the end in the performance of duty. Fortitude imparts to the will an impulse and energy which move it to undertake without hesitancy the most arduous tasks, to face dangers, to trample underfoot human respect, and to endure without complaint the slow martyrdom of even lifelong tribulation. “He that shall persevere unto the end, he shall be saved.”
Prayer:
Come, O Blessed Spirit of Fortitude, uphold my soul in time of trouble and adversity, sustain my efforts after holiness, strengthen my weakness, give me courage against all the assaults of my enemies, that I may never be overcome and separated from Thee, my God and greatest Good. Amen.
Close with: Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.
DAY 5 - May 10
Event title: Novena for JP - Day 5: Gift of Knowledge
Light immortal, light divine, visit Thou these hearts of Thine, and our inmost being fill.
Meditation:
The gift of Knowledge enables the soul to evaluate created things at their true worth - in their relation to God. Knowledge unmasks the pretense of creatures, reveals their emptiness, and points out their only true purpose as instruments in the service of God. It shows us the loving care of God even in adversity, and directs us to glorify Him in every circumstance of life. Guided by its light, we put first things first, and prize the friendship of God beyond all else. “Knowledge is a fountain of life to him that possesseth it.”
Prayer:
Come, O Blessed Spirit of Knowledge, and grant that I may perceive the will of the Father; show me the nothingness of earthly things, that I may realize their vanity and use them only for Thy glory and my own salvation, looking ever beyond them to Thee, and Thy eternal rewards. Amen.
Close with: Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.
DAY 6 - May 11
Event title: Novena for JP - Day 6: Gift of Understanding
If Thou take Thy grace away, nothing pure in man will stay, all his good is turned to ill.
Meditation:
Understanding, as a gift of the Holy Ghost, helps us to grasp the meaning of the truths of our holy religion. By faith we know them, but by Understanding we learn to appreciate and relish them. It enables us to penetrate the inner meaning of revealed truths and through them to be quickened to newness of life. Our faith ceases to be sterile and inactive, but inspires a mode of life that bears eloquent testimony to the faith that is in us; we begin to “walk worthy of God in all things pleasing, and increasing in the knowledge of God.”
Prayer:
Come, O Spirit of Understanding, and enlighten our minds, that we may know and believe all the mysteries of salvation; and may merit at last to see the eternal light in Thy Light; and in the light of glory to have a clear vision of Thee and the Father and the Son. Amen.
Close with: Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.
DAY 7 - May 12
Event title: Novena for JP - Day 7: Gift of Counsel
Heal our wounds, our strength renew; on our dryness pour Thy dew, wash the stains of guilt away.
Meditation:
The gift of Counsel endows the soul with supernatural prudence, enabling it to judge promptly and rightly what must be done, especially in difficult circumstances. Counsel applies the principles furnished by Knowledge and Understanding to the innumerable concrete cases that confront us in the course of our daily duty as parents, teachers, public servants, and Christian citizens. Counsel is supernatural common sense, a priceless treasure in the quest of salvation. “Above all these things, pray to the Most High, that He may direct thy way in truth.”
Prayer:
Come, O Spirit of Counsel, help and guide me in all my ways, that I may always do Thy holy will. Incline my heart to that which is good; turn it away from all that is evil, and direct me by the straight path of Thy commandments to that goal of eternal life for which I long. Amen.
Close with: Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.
DAY 8 - May 13
Event title: Novena for JP - Day 8: Gift of Wisdom
Bend the stubborn heart and will, melt the frozen, warm the chill, guide the steps that go astray.
Meditation:
Embodying all the other gifts, as charity embraces all the other virtues, Wisdom is the most perfect of the gifts. Of Wisdom it is written: “All good things came to me with her, and innumerable riches through her hands.” It is the gift of Wisdom that strengthens our faith, fortifies hope, perfects charity, and promotes the practice of virtue in the highest degree. Wisdom enlightens the mind to discern and relish things divine, in the appreciation of which earthly joys lose their savor, whilst the Cross of Christ yields a divine sweetness according to the words of the Savior: “Take up thy cross and follow me, for my yoke is sweet and my burden light.”
Prayer:
Come, O Spirit of Wisdom, and reveal to my soul the mysteries of heavenly things, their exceeding greatness, power and beauty. Teach me to love them above and beyond all the passing joys and satisfactions of earth. Help me to attain them and possess them forever. Amen.
Close with: Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.
DAY 9 - May 14
Event title: Novena for JP - Day 9: The Fruits of the Holy Ghost
Thou, on those who evermore Thee confess and Thee adore, in Thy sevenfold gift descend; give them comfort when they die; give them life with Thee on high; give them joys which never end. Amen.
Meditation:
The gifts of the Holy Ghost perfect the supernatural virtues by enabling us to practice them with greater docility to divine inspiration. As we grow in the knowledge and love of God under the direction of the Holy Ghost, our service becomes more sincere and generous, the practice of virtue more perfect. Such acts of virtue leave the heart filled with joy and consolation and are known as Fruits of the Holy Ghost. These Fruits in turn render the practice of virtue more attractive and become a powerful incentive for still greater efforts in the service of God, to serve Whom is to reign.
Prayer:
Come, O Divine Spirit, fill my heart with Thy heavenly fruits: Thy charity, joy, peace, patience, benignity, goodness, faith, mildness, and temperance, that I may never weary in the service of God, but by continued faithful submission to Thy inspiration may merit to be united eternally with Thee in the love of the Father and the Son. Amen.
Close with: Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.

Wisdom enlightens the mind to discern and relish things divine, in the appreciation of which earthly joys lose their savor, whilst the Cross of Christ yields a divine sweetness according to the words of the Savior: 'Take up thy cross and follow me, for my yoke is sweet and my burden light.'

Prayer:
Come, O Spirit of Wisdom, and reveal to my soul the mysteries of heavenly things, their exceeding greatness, power and beauty. Teach me to love them above and beyond all the passing joys and satisfactions of earth. Help me to attain them and possess them forever. Amen.

Reminder: Close with Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.

events.title: Novena for JP - Day 9: The Fruits of the Holy Ghost
events.notes:
Thou, on those who evermore Thee confess and Thee adore, in Thy sevenfold gift descend; give them comfort when they die; give them life with Thee on high; give them joys which never end. Amen.

Meditation:
The gifts of the Holy Ghost perfect the supernatural virtues by enabling us to practice them with greater docility to divine inspiration. As we grow in the knowledge and love of God under the direction of the Holy Ghost, our service becomes more sincere and generous, the practice of virtue more perfect. Such acts of virtue leave the heart filled with joy and consolation and are known as Fruits of the Holy Ghost. These Fruits in turn render the practice of virtue more attractive and become a powerful incentive for still greater efforts in the service of God, to serve Whom is to reign.

Prayer:
Come, O Divine Spirit, fill my heart with Thy heavenly fruits: Thy charity, joy, peace, patience, benignity, goodness, faith, mildness, and temperance, that I may never weary in the service of God, but by continued faithful submission to Thy inspiration may merit to be united eternally with Thee in the love of the Father and the Son. Amen.

Reminder: Close with Our Father once, Hail Mary once, Glory Be seven times, Act of Consecration, Prayer for the Seven Gifts.
